title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract from the plant's flowers, known as Echinacea pallida, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, including in the eyes. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate dry eye symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower tinctures can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing discomfort and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kansas Coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms such as redness and irritation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in Kansas Coneflower tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye disease.

Immunomodulatory: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune response in the eyes, reducing inflammation and preventing excessive mucus production that can further exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote the production of tears, thereby alleviating dry eyes.
Alkaloids (e.g., Cichoric Acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing irritation and dryness.
Phenolic Acids (e.g., Chlorogenic Acid): These antioxidants may help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healthy tear production and reducing dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.

Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.

Barks: Echinacea angustifolia barks are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their high content of phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in autumn when the plant is in full maturity.
Chop 1 part of the roots into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of vodka or glycerin.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with dry eyes because its extracts contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tannins and flavonoids in maidenhair tree work to reduce redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry eye syndrome. Additionally, the tincture's ability to improve ocular blood flow helps to nourish and hydrate the eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The tincture may help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair tree tincture may reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye disease.
Tissue-protective properties: Some studies suggest that Adiantum extracts may have protective effects on corneal and conjunctival tissues, potentially helping to repair and maintain ocular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds, particularly quercetin, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating dry eye symptoms.
Bilobalide: As a ginkgolide, bilobalide has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, contributing to relief from dry eye conditions.

Ginkgolides (including Ginkgolide A): These terpenoid lactones possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to the alleviation of dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce symptoms of dry eyes.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used to create tinctures due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to alleviate dry, itchy eyes.

Fruits: Ginkgo fruits are used to make tinctures because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, making them beneficial for dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of ginkgo biloba leaves and stems from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the ginkgo biloba material in a warm oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Crush 1 pound of dried ginkgo biloba material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 pound of ginkgo biloba powder with 5 cups of 80-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated eye tissues. The tincture's high concentration of allantoin reduces redness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by prolonged screen use or environmental factors. As a result, comfrey tinctures provide fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, and tired eyes, promoting healthy vision and overall ocular well-being.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: This compound helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes, which can alleviate dryness and discomfort caused by conditions like blepharitis or meibomian gland dysfunction.

Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, which can provide relief from redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids are antioxidants that help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms such as blurred vision and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, Symphytine and Luteolin): These alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation.

Flavonoids (specifically, Kaempferol and Quercetin): These flavonoids have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating dryness and discomfort.

Sterols (specifically, Sitosterol): These sterols have emollient and anti-inflammatory properties that help moisturize and protect the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes.
Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to stimulate the production of mucous, which can help to lubricate and protect dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale at dawn or early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Clean the harvested leaves by rinsing them gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned symphytum officinale leaves with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The natural gel of aloe vera has been used for centuries to treat various eye issues, including dryness and itchiness. When applied as a tincture, the aloe's moisturizing and regenerative effects penetrate deep into the eye, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and dryness. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can exacerbate dry eyes, promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate dry eye symptoms like redness and swelling.
Moisturizing: The gel-like substance present in herbal aloe tinctures helps to moisturize and lubricate the eyes, providing instant relief from dryness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic dry eye conditions and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the ocular surface, which can alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Glycoproteins: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce irritation and soothe the dry, itchy eyes, providing relief from dry eye discomfort.
Fatty acids (specifically, linoleic acid): Help to repair and maintain the integrity of the tear film, promoting healthy tears and reducing dryness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from aloe vera leaves is commonly used to make tinctures for dry eyes because of its soothing and moisturizing properties.
Leaves (Aloe vera gel extract): It contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
Leaves (Aloe vera inner gel): The inner gel is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which help protect and nourish the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh aloe vera leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest potency.
Wash the aloe vera leaves in cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the aloe vera leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 200 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol with the dried aloe vera leaves in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks then strain and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When used topically, goldenseal tincture reduces inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dry eye syndrome such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to dry eye conditions. By soothing and healing the eyes, goldenseal tinctures provide relief from discomfort and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of goldenseal, has antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and infection in the eyes, which may contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Sennosides: The sennoside content in goldenseal tincture acts as a natural demulcent, coating the eyes with a protective film that soothes and moisturizes the surface, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
Berberin's anti-inflammatory effects: Berberine has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes associated with dry eye syndrome.

Alkaloids (primarily berberine and hydrastine): Berberine has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent infections and promote healing in the eyes, while hydrastine may help reduce inflammation and promote mucin production to soothe dry eyes.

Flavonoids (primarily quercetin): Quercetin is a potent antioxidant that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, promoting healthy eye function and reducing the risk of dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis contain berberine, a compound that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in hydrastine, a compound that can help to reduce dryness and irritation in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain berberine and hydrastine, which can help to soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of hydrastis canadensis roots in the fall or spring when the plant is actively growing.
Dry the roots in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit for 8-12 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves by pinching or cutting them from mature plants.

Combine the cleaned plant material with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar for tincture preparation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried flowers of hypericum perforatum in the morning after dew has evaporated.

Steep 1 part of the chopped flowers in 5 parts of a solvent such as glycerin or ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 to 6 weeks in a clean glass jar.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: South American yerba tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achyrocline satureioides leaves in the early morning when they are most potent and fragrant, using clean scissors or pruning shears.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cold distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine 500 grams of dried achyrocline satureioides leaves with 1 liter of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged in the solvent.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a dark place at room temperature, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction and mixing, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool, dark location, allowing it to mature for at least 2 weeks before use.

Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration to preserve its medicinal properties.
Chop 200g of dried foeniculum vulgare into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with 500ml of high-proof vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it, using it within six months or longer with proper storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Porter's lovage tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. Lovage, a member of the parsley family, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated tissues, including the eyes. When taken as a tincture, its active constituents are absorbed into the bloodstream and help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning associated with dry eye syndrome. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for those who experience frequent dryness or discomfort due to environmental factors or other health conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lovage tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that may reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Lovage tincture can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Soil and demulcent properties: Lovage tincture contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can soothe and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them an effective remedy for dry eye relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's antimicrobial properties aid in reducing inflammation and infections that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms, promoting a healthy ocular environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ligusticum porteri roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Cut the washed roots into small pieces and combine them with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the root-vodka mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar weekly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Store the resulting tincture in an amber bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature away from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with dry eyes because its rich antioxidant properties soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing inflammation and redness. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory compounds also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. Additionally, mullein's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, locking in moisture and reducing evaporation, providing long-lasting relief from dry, itchy, and gritty eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mullein tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome, making it easier to manage symptoms like blurry vision and sensitivity to light.
Antioxidant: As an antioxidant, mullein tincture helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye disease, and promotes overall ocular health.

Soothing mucilage production: Mullein's soothing properties stimulate the production of mucilage in the eyes, helping to lubricate and moisturize the cornea and conjunctiva, alleviating dryness and itchiness associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate eye irritation and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers and dry them completely in a warm place.
Measure out 100 grams of dried herb and combine it with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol.
Steep the herb mixture in the liquid for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the finished tincture in the refrigerator for up to two years, using 10-20 drops diluted in water three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains a unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and nourish the delicate tissues around the eyes. The herbal extract also improves blood circulation to the eyes, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to dryness and irritation. Additionally, beach rose has natural lubricating properties that help to replenish the tear film, leaving eyes feeling moist, comfortable, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Beach rose tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the eye, which can help alleviate dry, irritated eyes and prevent further damage.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in beach rose tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress caused by UV rays, pollution, and other environmental factors that can contribute to dry eyes.
Soothing and calming properties: Beach rose tincture has a calming effect on the eyes, which can help reduce redness, itchiness, and irritation associated with dry eyes, providing instant relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve ocular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and quality.
Cleanly sort and rinse the flowers removing any debris or stems to ensure purity.
Combine 100 grams of dried rosa rugosa flowers with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for eight hours allowing the tincture to extract and mature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and label accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts contain powerful compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, soothing dryness and irritation. Additionally, highbush cranberry's antioxidant-rich profile protects the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tear production and overall ocular health. As a natural remedy, highbush cranberry tincture can provide effective relief for dry eye symptoms, making it a valuable addition to any natural eye care routine.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is often a underlying cause of dryness, redness, and irritation.
Vasodilatory properties: Highbush cranberry's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood flow to the eyes, which may increase tear production and reduce symptoms of dry eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are effective in reducing inflammation and protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes.
Barks: The barks contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, providing relief from dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries or 2 ounces of dried berries.
Chop the berries into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding solids.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles and label with date of preparation and recommended dosage of 20-40 drops up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in elderberries have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from dry, itchy, and gritty eyes. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in elder tincture help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms. By promoting healthy tear production and reducing oxidative stress, elder tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for managing dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome by reducing the risk of damage to the corneal epithelium.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye symptoms like redness, itchiness, and burning.

Muco-regenerative properties: Elder tincture's mucilage content helps stimulate the production of mucins, which are essential for maintaining the health and function of the ocular surface, including the conjunctiva and cornea, thereby helping to alleviate dry eye symptoms like dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in bioactive compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them an excellent choice for dry eye relief.
Barks: The barks of the elder tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and quality.
Cleanly chop the flowers into small pieces to facilitate maceration and solvent penetration.
Combine one part of chopped sambucus nigra flowers with three parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily, to ensure optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters into a clean amber glass bottle, discarding solids and preserving in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry eyes are liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate dry, irritated, and itchy eyes by providing moisture and reducing inflammation. They work by soothing and hydrating the eyes, relieving dryness and discomfort. The best herbal tinctures for dry eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Aloe vera, Althaea officinalis, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, dry eyes can lead to chronic discomfort, blurred vision, and even eye infections, severely impacting daily activities and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are various forms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbs like golden seal, calendula, and chamomile can help soothe irritated eyes, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Other herbs like peppermint, tea tree, and lavender can also provide relief from dryness, itching, and redness associated with these conditions. Tinctures can be applied topically or taken orally to provide effective natural remedies for eye health issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with dry eyes is often a blend of Nettle Leaf (Urtica dioica), Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis), and Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). Nettle Leaf helps to soothe and moisturize the eyes, while Eyebright reduces inflammation and calms irritation. Goldenseal adds antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote healing. Taking a few drops of this blend several times a day may provide relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the production of tears and promote ocular health. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry eyes. Additionally, its ability to soothe and calm the conjunctiva, the membrane that covers the white part of the eye, helps to alleviate discomfort and promote healing. By addressing the root causes of dry eyes, Euphrasia officinalis tinctures offer a natural and effective solution for this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and improving the health of the ocular surface.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: They help with dry eyes by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which protect the eyes from further damage.
Flavonoids: They help with dry eyes by reducing oxidative stress and improving the health of the cornea and conjunctiva.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are also used extensively due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
Stems: The stems of Euphrasia officinalis contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote eye health, making them a popular choice for tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in mid-morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for about 2 weeks to achieve 10% moisture.
Combine 100 grams of dried plant material with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter and transfer it to a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes and eyelids, Calendula officinalis tinctures help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome. This makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in soothing and lubricating dry eyes.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate dry eye symptoms and promote overall eye health.
Phenylpropanoids (specifically caffeic acid): They have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and swelling in the eyes, contributing to relief from dry eye discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated eyes.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting eye health.
Buds: May be used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, although less common than flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula flowers when they are in full bloom typically between june and august.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Use a 1:2 or 1:3 parts of plant material to solvent ratio with 80 proof vodka as the solvent.
Steep the dried calendula flowers in the solvent for 2-6 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The herb, also known as comfrey, has been traditionally used to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the eyes. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture help to reduce inflammation and promote the production of mucin, a protective layer that helps to retain moisture in the eyes. This can provide relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for dry eye syndrome.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: Help with dry eyes by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the eyes, promoting healthy tear production and reducing inflammation.
Flavonoids: Contribute to the relief of dry eyes by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to maintain the health and integrity of the ocular surface.
Allantoin: Aid in the alleviation of dry eyes by promoting epithelial cell regeneration and wound healing, thus supporting the recovery and maintenance of the eye's mucous membranes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of mucilages, which help to soothe and moisturize the eyes.
Roots: The roots are also used, as they contain similar mucilages that help to calm and lubricate the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are sometimes used, but less frequently than leaves and roots, as they contain smaller amounts of mucilages, but still contribute to the tincture's soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the fresh leaves in a single layer at 25c for 24 to 48 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried symphytum officinale leaves with 500 milliliters of 35% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated eyes. The tincture's immunomodulatory effects may also help regulate the immune system's response to dry eye triggers, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties may protect the eyes from oxidative stress, further contributing to its potential benefits in alleviating dry eye symptoms, promoting healthy vision, and improving overall eye comfort. This makes it a promising natural remedy for dry eye sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce the severity of dry eye symptoms.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, contributing to the alleviation of dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots contain compounds like alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain volatile oils and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and promote eye health, making them a useful addition to dry eye tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Chop the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part of chopped echinacea purpurea with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every week.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant, also known as yarrow, contains compounds that soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation. The tincture's ability to penetrate deep into the eyes helps to replenish moisture and promote healthy tear production, providing relief from dry, itchy, and uncomfortable eyes. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce redness and puffiness, promoting a more comfortable and healthy eye environment.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation.
Nepetalactone: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce discomfort and inflammation associated with dry eyes.
Cichoriin: It has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.
Stems: The stems of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning when dew is present and flowers are fully open.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or by using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1 part dried achillea millefolium plant material with 2 parts high-quality vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to enhance extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with dry eyes because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture may help to reduce inflammation and promote the health of the meibomian glands, which are responsible for producing the oily layer of the tear film. This can lead to improved tear quality and reduced dryness, providing relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with dry eyes.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of tears, thereby alleviating dryness and discomfort.
Isolation of triterpenoid saponin: It helps with dry eyes by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.
Phenolic acids (such as flavonoids): They help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting the health of the ocular surface, and improving tear quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry, irritated eyes.

Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures due to their ability to provide sustained release of glycyrrhizin, which can help maintain moisture and reduce dryness in the eyes over an extended period.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated and the sun is not too strong.
Clean the roots thoroughly with a brush to remove dirt and debris then soak them in water for hours.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts vodka.
Place the mixture in a glass jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids then store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with dry eyes because they improve blood circulation to the eyes, increasing oxygen supply and nutrient delivery to the ocular tissues. This enhanced circulation can also help reduce inflammation and promote the health of the tear glands. As a result, the eyes are better able to produce and maintain a healthy tear film, alleviating symptoms of dry eyes such as itchiness, redness, and blurred vision. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with dry eyes by improving blood flow to the eyes and reducing inflammation, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome.
Quercetin: This flavonoid helps with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, and also by improving tear quality, which can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba in tinctures for dry eyes due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in tinctures for dry eyes because they contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye irritation.

Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba are used in tinctures for dry eyes due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to soothe and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in a clean glass container for later use.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of 60 percent ethanol in a large glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Transfer the tincture into dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tincture is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and magnesium, which help to soothe and hydrate the eyes. By reducing inflammation and replenishing moisture, aloe vera tinctures can alleviate dry eye symptoms such as irritation, redness, and blurred vision. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote healthy eye function. Regular use may help to maintain eye health and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Aloe vera tinctures have antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing of the ocular surface.
Sterols: Sterols, particularly beta-sitosterol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high concentration of soothing compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain mucilages, which are gel-like substances that help to moisturize and soothe dry, irritated eyes, making them a popular choice for tincture production.
Barks: The barks of Aloe vera contain various flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to protect the eyes from damage and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves in the morning after the plant has been in sunlight for several hours.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine with 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the aloe vera and ethanol mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the aloe vera tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for several months before using as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its soothing and emollient properties. The root of the plant, Marshmallow, contains mucilages that help to moisturize and protect the eyes. When applied topically, it forms a protective barrier that shields the eyes from dryness and irritation. This natural remedy can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the ocular surface, providing relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes. Its anti-inflammatory properties also aid in soothing redness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with dry eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation.
Triterpenoids (including Althaea saponins): Triterpenoids help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the ocular surface, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Flavonoids help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms and damage to the ocular surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in mucilages, which help soothe and protect the eyes.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce eye irritation and inflammation.
Barks: They possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating dry, infected eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root material of althaea officinalis in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested root material in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to prevent mold growth.
Chop 1 part of dried root material into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. Regular use of aloe vera tincture can help to moisturize and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing dryness, itchiness, and irritation, and promoting a healthy and comfortable vision. This natural remedy can provide relief from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
Polysaccharides: They have moisturizing and soothing effects on the eyes, helping to restore the natural moisture balance and reduce dryness.
Glycoproteins: These constituents have been found to have a protective effect on the ocular surface, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis contain aloin and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes.
Stems: The stems of Aloe barbadensis contain aloe-emodin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye inflammation and promote healthy vision.

Roots: The roots of Aloe barbadensis contain a variety of compounds, including aloin and aloe-emodin, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from aloe barbadensis plants with a sharp knife for optimal potency.
Cut the harvested leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar for storage.
Pack the aloe pieces tightly into a glass jar and cover them with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
